models.name: Human Heart
models.description: The muscular organ that pumps blood through the circulatory system, beating roughly 100,000 times per day.
models.descriptionShort: 4-chambered pump of the circulatory system.

models.name: Human Skeleton
models.description: The framework of 206 bones that supports the body, protects organs, and enables movement.
models.descriptionShort: 206-bone framework of the human body.

models.name: Human Brain
models.description: The central organ of the nervous system, responsible for thought, memory, emotion, and coordination.
models.descriptionShort: Command center of the nervous system.

labels.name: Superior & Inferior colliculus (parts of the midbrain)

models.name: Human Skin
models.description: The largest organ of the body, forming a protective barrier and regulating temperature.
models.descriptionShort: Body's outermost protective layer.

models.name: Human Eye
models.description: The sensory organ responsible for vision, converting light into electrical signals sent to the brain.
models.descriptionShort: Light-sensing organ of the visual system.

models.name: Human Lungs
models.description: The primary organs of the respiratory system, responsible for gas exchange between air and blood.
models.descriptionShort: Gas exchange organs of the respiratory system.

models.name: Female Reproductive System
models.description: The female reproductive organs including the uterus, ovaries, fallopian tubes, and associated structures.
models.descriptionShort: Female reproductive organs and structures.

models.name: Urinary System
models.description: The organ system that filters waste products from the blood and excretes them as urine.
models.descriptionShort: Kidney-led system for waste filtration and urine production.

models.name: Neuron
models.description: The fundamental unit of the nervous system that transmits electrical and chemical signals throughout the body.
models.descriptionShort: Basic signalling cell of the nervous system.

models.name: Life Cycle of a Butterfly
models.description: The complete metamorphosis of a butterfly through four stages: egg, larva (caterpillar), pupa (chrysalis), and adult.
models.descriptionShort: Four-stage metamorphosis: egg, caterpillar, chrysalis, butterfly.

models.name: Teeth
models.description: The hard calcified structures in the mouth used for biting and chewing food, including incisors, canines, and molars.
models.descriptionShort: Hard structures in the mouth for biting and chewing.

models.name: Thyroid Gland
models.description: A butterfly-shaped endocrine gland in the neck that produces hormones regulating metabolism, growth, and development.
models.descriptionShort: Endocrine gland regulating metabolism and growth.

models.name: Lymphatic System
models.description: A network of tissues, organs, and vessels that maintain fluid balance, support immunity, and absorb dietary fats.
models.descriptionShort: Immune and fluid-balancing network of vessels and nodes.

models.name: Digestive System
models.description: The group of organs that break down food into nutrients the body can absorb, from mouth to large intestine.
models.descriptionShort: Organ system that breaks down food into nutrients.

models.name: Male Reproductive System
models.description: The organs involved in producing, storing, and delivering sperm for reproduction.
models.descriptionShort: Organs for sperm production and delivery.

models.name: Nephron
models.description: The microscopic functional unit of the kidney responsible for filtering blood and producing urine.
models.descriptionShort: Kidney's microscopic filtration unit.

models.name: Animal Cell
models.description: The basic structural and functional unit of animal life, containing a nucleus and various organelles.
models.descriptionShort: Basic unit of animal life with organelles.

models.name: Bacteria
models.description: Single-celled prokaryotic microorganisms found in nearly every habitat on Earth.
models.descriptionShort: Single-celled prokaryotic microorganism.

models.name: DNA
models.description: The double-helix molecule that carries the genetic instructions for the development, functioning, and reproduction of all living organisms.
models.descriptionShort: Double-helix molecule carrying genetic code.

models.name: Plant Cell
models.description: The basic structural unit of plant life, distinguished by a cell wall, chloroplasts, and a large central vacuole.
models.descriptionShort: Basic unit of plant life with cell wall and chloroplasts.

models.name: Anatomy of a Flower
models.description: The structure of a flower including petals, sepals, stamens, and pistil, which are the reproductive parts of a plant.
models.descriptionShort: Structural parts of a flower including reproductive organs.

models.name: Atom
models.description: A basic atomic model showing the nucleus made of protons and neutrons, with electrons orbiting in shells.
models.descriptionShort: Nucleus of protons & neutrons with orbiting electrons.

models.name: Layers of Earth
models.description: The internal structure of the Earth comprising the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core.
models.descriptionShort: Earth's internal layers from crust to core.

models.name: Soil Profile
models.description: The vertical cross-section of soil showing distinct layers called horizons from surface to bedrock.
models.descriptionShort: Layered cross-section of soil horizons.

models.name: Volcano
models.description: A geological formation where magma from beneath the Earth's crust erupts through the surface.
models.descriptionShort: Magma eruption point in Earth's crust.

models.name: Solar System
models.description: Our planetary system consisting of the Sun and the eight planets, moons, asteroids, and comets orbiting it.
models.descriptionShort: The Sun and its orbiting planets.
